signals.headline: US signs landmark nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia
signals.summary: The US and Saudi Arabia have signed a landmark deal that will reportedly allow the Gulf kingdom to develop a civilian nuclear programme.
keyTakeaways: This could be worth billions of dollars to the US, whose companies could be involved in building a facility to produce enriched uranium, which can be used as fuel for power plants but also to make nuclear bombs.

According to Wednesday's statement, the deal will "advance US and regional security by upholding high standards of nuclear safety, security, and nonproliferation and strengthening the United States' competitive edge in civil nuclear technology".
Nuclear experts and former officials in Israel - the US's top ally in the Middle East - voiced concern the plan could ultimately lead to Saudi Arabia being able to develop a nuclear weapon.
signals.whyItMatters: The deal is described as a "peaceful nuclear co-operation agreement" that will provide "great access for American companies in the Saudi nuclear energy program", according to a statement from the US energy secretary.

ja.headline: 米国とサウジアラビア、民間核開発で歴史的合意
ja.summary: 米国とサウジアラビアは、サウジが民間の核開発プログラムを進めることを認める歴史的な協定に署名した。
keyTakeaways: 米企業がウラン濃縮施設の建設に関与する可能性があり、協定は数十億ドル規模の経済効果をもたらすとみられる。
声明によれば、協定は核の安全・不拡散の高い基準を維持しつつ、米国の民間核技術における競争力を強化するとしている。
中東における米国の最重要同盟国であるイスラエルの核専門家や元当局者からは、サウジが将来的に核兵器開発に至る可能性があるとの懸念が上がっている。
ja.whyItMatters: 米エネルギー長官の声明によれば、この協定は「平和的な原子力協力協定」と位置づけられ、サウジの核エネルギー計画への米企業の参入を広く認めるものだという。

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: So the US just signed a nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Yes, it's described as a peaceful nuclear cooperation agreement. It allows Saudi Arabia to develop a civilian nuclear programme.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: What does that actually mean for the US?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: It could be worth billions of dollars. American companies would get access to help build facilities, including ones that produce enriched uranium for power plants.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: Wait, enriched uranium... isn't that also used for weapons?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: It can be. The US says the deal upholds high standards of nuclear safety and nonproliferation, and strengthens American competitive edge in civil nuclear technology.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: Are people worried about that weapons possibility?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Some are. Nuclear experts and former officials in Israel have voiced concern the plan could ultimately lead to Saudi Arabia being able to develop a nuclear weapon.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: So there's a tension between the business opportunity and the security risk.

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Exactly. The US is emphasizing regional security and nonproliferation standards, but the concern remains.

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: アメリカとサウジアラビアが、サウジの民間向け原子力開発を認める協定に署名しました。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: この協定で、アメリカ企業がサウジ国内のウラン濃縮施設の建設に関わる道が開かれます。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: ウラン濃縮は発電所の燃料として使われますが、核兵器の材料にもなり得る技術です。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: アメリカ政府は、この協定が核の安全と不拡散の高い基準を守りながら、自国の民間原子力技術の競争力を高めると説明しています。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 一方で、イスラエルの専門家や元政府関係者からは懸念の声が上がっています。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 将来サウジが核兵器を持つ可能性につながるのではないか、という見方です。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 協定の規模は数十億ドルに達するとみられ、アメリカ企業にとって大きな商機となります。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 中東の安全保障の構図が、静かに変わり始めているのかもしれません。

signals.headline: Ukraine's Zelenskyy fires top military commander amid ongoing protests
signals.summary: Ukraine's President Volodymyr Zelenskyy fires the country's army chief, just days after he dismissed the defense minister.
keyTakeaways: LEILA FADEL, HOST: Ukraine's president, Volodymyr Zelenskyy, has now fired the country's top military commander.
Just a few days ago, Zelenskyy fired the country's defense minister.
FADEL: So in less than a week, President Zelenskyy fired the two top figures leading Ukraine's war against Russia.
signals.whyItMatters: Together, it's one of the most significant military shakeups in the past four years of Ukraine's war with Russia.

ja.headline: ゼレンスキー大統領、軍トップを相次いで更迭
ja.summary: ウクライナのゼレンスキー大統領が、国軍の総司令官を解任した。国防相の更迭からわずか数日後のことだ。
keyTakeaways: ゼレンスキー大統領が、ウクライナ軍の最高司令官を解任した。
その数日前には、国防相もすでに更迭されていた。
1週間も経たないうちに、戦時下の軍を率いる二人の最高幹部が相次いで交代することになった。
ja.whyItMatters: ロシアとの戦争が続く4年間で、これほど大規模な軍の指導部刷新は前例がない。

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: I saw that Ukraine's president just fired his top military commander. What's happening there?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: President Zelenskyy removed the country's army chief from his position. This comes just days after he also dismissed the defense minister.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: So both the top military leaders are out in less than a week?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Yes. These were the two top figures leading Ukraine's war effort against Russia.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: That seems like a pretty big deal during an ongoing war.

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: It is. This is being called one of the most significant military shakeups in the past four years of the war.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: Do we know why he made these changes now?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: The reporting doesn't specify the reasons behind the dismissals. What we know is the timing and that protests are ongoing in the country.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: That's a lot of uncertainty at a critical moment.

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: It is. Leadership changes like this during wartime always raise questions about strategy and direction going forward.

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: ウクライナのゼレンスキー大統領が、軍の総司令官を解任しました。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: その数日前には国防相も更迭されていて、1週間足らずで軍の最高幹部2人が職を失ったことになります。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: ロシアとの戦争が始まって4年になりますが、これほど大きな軍の指導部の入れ替えは初めてです。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 戦時下で軍を率いる立場の人事が短期間に動くのは、異例のことです。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 背景には国内での抗議活動も続いていて、政権への圧力が高まっている様子がうかがえます。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 今後、新しい指導部がどのように戦況に向き合うのか、注目されます。

signals.headline: British woman jailed in Hong Kong for blackmail after accusing banker of rape
signals.summary: A British woman who reported being raped to police in Hong Kong has been sentenced to six years in prison for blackmail and perverting the course of public justice.
keyTakeaways: Isabel Rose, 26, from south-east London, travelled to Hong Kong in early 2024 to visit a British banker she had met while travelling in south-east Asia.
The Hong Kong district judge Adriana Noelle Tse Ching said Rose had “not shown an iota of remorse” before handing down the sentence, and called the allegation “completely fabricated”.
Rose travelled to Hong Kong in January 2024 after meeting the banker on holiday in Thailand.
signals.whyItMatters: The judge said the escalating demands amounted to blackmail.

ja.headline: レイプ被害を訴えた英国人女性、香港で恐喝罪により実刑判決
ja.summary: 香港警察にレイプ被害を申告した英国人女性が、恐喝および司法妨害の罪で懲役6年の実刑判決を受けた。
keyTakeaways: ロンドン南東部出身のイザベル・ローズ（26歳）は、東南アジア旅行中に知り合った英国人銀行家を訪ねて2024年初頭に香港へ渡った。
香港地方裁判所のアドリアナ・ノエル・謝靖裁判官は、ローズが「一切の反省を示さなかった」と述べ、申告内容を「完全な虚偽」と断じたうえで量刑を言い渡した。
ローズはタイでの休暇中にその銀行家と出会い、2024年1月に香港を訪れていた。
ja.whyItMatters: 裁判官は、金銭要求がエスカレートしていった経緯が恐喝にあたると判断した。

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: There's a troubling case out of Hong Kong involving a British woman. What happened?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: A 26-year-old woman from south-east London, Isabel Rose, has been sentenced to six years in prison there.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: What was she convicted of?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Blackmail and perverting the course of public justice. She had accused a British banker of rape after travelling to Hong Kong to visit him.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: So the court didn't believe her accusation?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: The judge called the allegation completely fabricated. She said Rose had not shown any remorse.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: How did they know each other?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: They'd met while travelling in south-east Asia — she was on holiday in Thailand. She travelled to Hong Kong in January 2024 to visit him.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: What led to the blackmail charge?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: The judge said Rose made escalating demands, which amounted to blackmail.

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 香港で、英国人女性が懲役6年の判決を受けました。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 女性は警察にレイプ被害を訴えていましたが、裁判所は恐喝と司法妨害の罪を認定しました。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 26歳のイザベル・ローズは、タイ旅行中に知り合った英国人銀行家を訪ねて香港へ渡っていました。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 裁判官は、彼女の訴えを完全な虚偽と判断し、金銭要求が段階的に高まった点を恐喝と認めました。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: ローズは一切の反省を示さなかったと、判決の場で指摘されています。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 虚偽の申告が重い刑罰につながった事例として、記録に残ることになります。

signals.headline: Price-hiked iPads are a little cheaper right now
signals.summary: This model sports 1TB of storage, an optional 5G cellular connection, and it’s marked down to $749, a $500 discount from the suggested retail price.
keyTakeaways: Sony is selling refurbished Playstation 5 Slim consoles for $499, a $100 break from the usual price.
Normally $99.99, a four pack of the second-generation Apple AirTags are on sale for $89.99 at Amazon, matching their lowest price ever.
Amazon has the Razer Viper V3 Pro wireless gaming mouse listed for $99.99, about $30 lower than its usual price.
signals.whyItMatters: They may have some minor cosmetic imperfections, but are tested and certified by Sony to perform just as well as a new console.

ja.headline: 値上がりしたiPadが、いま少し手に届きやすい価格に
ja.summary: 1TBストレージと5G接続オプションを備えたこのモデルが、希望小売価格から500ドル引きの749ドルで販売されている。
keyTakeaways: ソニーが整備済みのPlayStation 5 Slimを499ドルで販売中。通常より100ドル安い。
第2世代AirTag 4個パックがAmazonで89.99ドルに。過去最安値に並ぶ水準だ。
Razer Viper V3 ProワイヤレスゲーミングマウスがAmazonで99.99ドル。通常より約30ドル低い。
ja.whyItMatters: 外観に軽微な傷がある場合もあるが、ソニーが動作を検査・認定しており、新品と同等の性能が保証されている。

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: I saw something about iPads being on sale. What's the deal?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: There's a model with a terabyte of storage and optional 5G that's marked down to seven forty-nine. That's five hundred dollars off the suggested retail price.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: Five hundred off? That's a big discount. Are there other tech deals happening right now?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Yes, a few. Sony is selling refurbished PlayStation 5 Slim consoles for four ninety-nine, which is a hundred dollars less than usual.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: Refurbished though—does that mean there's something wrong with them?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: They may have minor cosmetic imperfections, but Sony tests and certifies them to perform just as well as new consoles.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: Got it. What else is discounted?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Apple AirTags are on sale—a four pack of the second generation is eighty-nine ninety-nine at Amazon, matching their lowest price ever. And there's a Razer gaming mouse for about thirty dollars off.

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: Appleの大容量iPadが、いま通常より大幅に安く買えるようになっています。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 1テラバイトの保存容量と5G通信に対応したモデルが、定価から500ドル引きの749ドルです。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: ソニーは整備済みのプレイステーション5スリムを499ドルで出していて、これは通常より100ドル安い価格です。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 見た目に小さな傷があることもありますが、ソニーが検査して性能を保証しています。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: アップルのエアタグ第2世代の4個セットは、アマゾンで89.99ドルに下がっていて、これまでで最も安い水準です。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: レイザーのワイヤレスゲーミングマウス、ヴァイパーV3プロも、通常より30ドルほど安い99.99ドルで手に入ります。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 値上げが続くなかで、こうした割引は買い替えを考えている人にとって貴重な機会になりそうです。

signals.headline: Tesla’s revenues are bouncing back, but profits are still weak
signals.summary: Tesla is in negative free cash flow territory, as it increases its expenditures on major projects like AI infrastructure and robotics.
keyTakeaways: After a dismal two years of weakening demand, falling sales, and damage to its brand by Elon Musk’s political activities, Tesla’s road to recovery continues apace.
Despite that mission, Tesla remains a car company.
Tesla certainly did a good job shrinking its inventory, which is good for the balance sheet.
signals.whyItMatters: In a shareholder deck, Tesla said it “generated over $100B in revenue on a trailing twelve-month basis for the first time.” It also touted Cybercab production at its Gigafactory in Texas, and said Tesla Semi production “remains on track” at its Nevada facility later this year.

ja.headline: テスラ、売上は回復基調も利益は依然低迷
ja.summary: テスラはAIインフラやロボティクスといった大型プロジェクトへの投資を拡大しており、フリーキャッシュフローはマイナスの状態が続いている。
keyTakeaways: 需要の低迷、販売不振、そしてイーロン・マスクの政治的言動によるブランドへのダメージと、苦難の2年間を経て、テスラの回復は着実に進んでいる。
とはいえ、テスラの本業はあくまで自動車メーカーだ。
在庫の圧縮は順調に進んでおり、財務体質の改善に寄与している。
ja.whyItMatters: テスラは株主向け資料の中で、直近12か月の累計売上高が初めて1000億ドルを超えたと明らかにし、テキサスのギガファクトリーでのサイバーキャブ生産やネバダ州でのテスラ・セミの生産計画が順調に進んでいることも強調した。

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: So Tesla's doing better than it was?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Yes, revenues are bouncing back after two tough years of falling sales and weakening demand. They just passed a hundred billion dollars in revenue over the last twelve months for the first time.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: That sounds good. But the headline says profits are still weak?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Right. They're actually in negative free cash flow right now because they're spending heavily on big projects like AI infrastructure and robotics.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: So they're making more money but spending even more?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Exactly. They've also been working on reducing inventory, which helps their balance sheet. And they're moving ahead with production plans for the Cybercab in Texas and the Semi truck in Nevada later this year.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: Is Tesla still mainly a car company despite all these other projects?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Yes, despite everything else, Tesla remains a car company at its core.

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: Teslaの売上が、ここ2年ほどの落ち込みから持ち直してきました。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 直近12か月の合計で、初めて1000億ドルを超えたそうです。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: ただ、利益の面ではまだ弱さが残っています。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 背景には、AIやロボット開発への大きな投資があります。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: そのため、自由に使える現金の流れはマイナスが続いています。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: テキサスやネバダの工場では、新しい車の生産が進んでいるとのことです。

captions.speaker: narrator
captions.text: 売上の回復は見えても、収益力が戻るかは今後の課題になりそうです。
